storyline: Breakwave Tanker Shipping (BWET) tracks crude-oil tanker freight rates (~90% VLCC / TD3C futures, Gulf→China). It spiked on the 2026 Iran war and Strait-of-Hormuz closure, which choked Gulf oil-tanker lanes and sent VLCC day-rates to record highs (~$424k/day). The catalyst resolves when the conflict de-escalates (a ceasefire, or the Strait reopening) and rates roll over — here the agent exited on the Apr-10 two-week ceasefire (which then proved temporary). What we'd want: ride it while the war risk is live and exit when it resolves — not when coverage merely gets crowded (crowding is not thesis death).

seeds.title: One of the Top-Performing ETFs of 2026 So Far May Surprise You
seeds.snippet: Investors seeking oil-freight futures exposure may be drawn to the Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), which has nearly doubled in the first weeks of the year as tanker rates climb.

seeds.title: Strait of Hormuz closure sends oil-tanker rates to record highs - Breakwave Tanker ETF (BWET) the under-owned way to play it
seeds.snippet: With Iran blocking the Strait of Hormuz since Feb 28 after US-Israeli strikes, the benchmark VLCC rate to ship 2 million barrels of crude from the Gulf to China hit an all-time high of $423,736 per day - up more than 94% in a single session - as insurers dropped war-risk cover and 150-plus tankers anchored rather than transit. Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET), about 90% VLCC (TD3C) freight futures, is the purest and still-under-owned way to own the spike as the disruption is only beginning.

seeds.title: This little-known ETF is up over 600% amid U.S.-Iran war - a better trade than oil or energy stocks
seeds.snippet: The Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (BWET) has surged more than 600% year to date as the U.S.-Iran war and the Strait-of-Hormuz disruption drive tanker freight rates sharply higher. About 90% of the portfolio is TD3C futures - the cost of shipping crude on very large crude carriers from the Middle East to China - giving unlevered exposure to oil-tanker freight without a futures account. It is the best-performing ETF of 2026.

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.exit_case: none — Strait of Hormuz remains actively blocked, tanker rates still at record highs
BWET.assessment: Entry week. Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz since Feb 28 following US-Israeli strikes has driven VLCC rates to an all-time record of $423,736/day. This is a clean, datable, acute chokepoint shock with a direct pure-play vehicle (BWET) still described as 'under-owned.' Catalyst is fresh and ongoing — no resolution yet, no reopening, no ceasefire. Conviction high given the acute, specific, still-escalating nature of the crisis and the explicit tie to tanker economics.
BWET.exit_advice: Hold BWET while Hormuz remains blocked and VLCC rates stay at record levels; exit if strait reopens or rates normalize sharply.

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.assessment: This week's only news item (Pakistan KSE-100 rally) is unrelated to the Hormuz blockade or tanker market. No confirmation of ceasefire, reopening, or de-escalation, so the driving condition (Iran's blockade, record VLCC rates) is assumed unchanged. However, total silence on the actual catalyst for a full week warrants a conviction step-down per silence decay rules, while remaining live since nothing suggests resolution or reversal.
BWET.exit_advice: Hold BWET; no news this week on Strait of Hormuz status, tanker rates, or blockade resolution — driving condition (blockade) remains presumed in force absent any reopening/ceasefire report. Applying silence decay since no fresh trend coverage appeared.

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.exit_case: none — blockade still in effect, no ceasefire or reopening reported; only tangential energy-stock coverage this week
BWET.assessment: News this week only tangentially references Strait of Hormuz via oil majors' stock performance, with no direct update on blockade status, tanker rates, or BWET fundamentals. No evidence of reversal (no reopening, no ceasefire, no restored shipping flow). Driving condition presumed unchanged. Continued silence on the specific catalyst (VLCC rates, blockade duration, BWET-specific coverage) compounds the silence decay — stepping conviction down further from last week's already-reduced level. Still thesis_live since nothing indicates resolution.
BWET.exit_advice: Hold BWET; watch for any Hormuz reopening/ceasefire headlines or explicit tanker-rate updates to reset conviction upward, or a confirmed de-escalation to trigger exit

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.exit_case: none found; no news confirms Hormuz reopening or ceasefire, blockade status unconfirmed but not reversed
BWET.assessment: Third consecutive week with zero direct coverage of the Strait of Hormuz blockade, VLCC rates, or BWET fundamentals. This week's sole news item (US construction spending) is entirely unrelated. No evidence of reversal — no reopening, no ceasefire, no restored shipping flow through Hormuz. Per silence decay rules, conviction steps down further from last week's reduced level given three straight quiet weeks on the specific catalyst. Still thesis_live since driving condition (blockade, record tanker rates) is presumed unchanged absent any reversal signal, but this is now a fading, under-covered position at risk of falling out of the shortlist if silence persists.
BWET.exit_advice: Hold small; continue silence decay — exit only if reopening/ceasefire confirmed or if next check shows continued zero coverage pushing conviction to cull floor

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.exit_case: none — Trump reportedly willing to end war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz, meaning blockade persists even if war ends
BWET.assessment: Fourth week update: this is the first direct, on-topic news since entry. Report that Trump is willing to end the war WITHOUT reopening Hormuz is critical — it explicitly confirms the driving condition (Strait blockage, and by extension record VLCC rates) is NOT contingent on a ceasefire and may persist independently. This is fresh, relevant coverage (not off-topic macro noise like prior weeks), so conviction resets upward from the silence-decayed level, though not to entry-week highs given the news is indirect (no explicit rate/BWET figures) and introduces some uncertainty about war trajectory. No reversal, reopening, or restored shipping flow reported — the reverse: the blockade is framed as durable/separable from war outcome. Thesis remains live and the pure-play vehicle intact.
BWET.exit_advice: Hold BWET; blockade condition explicitly confirmed as separate from war resolution, so record tanker rates likely persist even if broader conflict winds down.

BWET.thesis: Iran blocks Strait of Hormuz, tanker rates hit record
BWET.exit_case: US-Iran agreed two-week ceasefire; markets rallying on expectations Strait of Hormuz reopens, crude falls.
BWET.assessment: After weeks of silence, this week brought decisive on-topic news: a Trump deadline for Iran to reopen Hormuz was followed almost immediately by a US-Iran ceasefire agreement, explicitly tied by markets to expectations that Strait energy flows resume (crude fell, global equities rallied on the news). This is a direct reversal of the acute driving condition that justified BWET's record-VLCC-rate thesis — unlike last week's report that Trump might accept a war-end without reopening Hormuz, this week's ceasefire is framed with explicit reopening expectations. Given the binary nature of the acute chokepoint catalyst (blocked vs. not blocked), a ceasefire with reopening expectations is a resolution signal, not mere quiet. Tanker rate premium built on blockade scarcity should compress rapidly regardless of whether the ceasefire ultimately holds long-term, since the market prices forward expectations. Thesis is no longer live; cull from shortlist.
BWET.exit_advice: Exit BWET — the driving condition (Hormuz blockade) has reversed via a negotiated ceasefire, with markets already pricing restored Strait flow and falling crude/tanker economics; the acute chokepoint thesis is broken even if the ceasefire proves temporary.
